rationale: Under the generality rubric, both reviews are defensible: the statements are already at the right abstraction level (field-generic, minimal hypotheses, Fintype variant exported), so Review 1's approval is sound. Review 2's most relevant finding (sqrtTower using ℕ-indexing) is contested since the ℕ structure enables the inductive proof and the general Fintype result is separately stated; its other findings concern proof strategy and API style, not generality of the stated results.
